title: Morphological Analysis of Pig Vagus Nerve Using Image Processing
author: Nicole A. Pelot
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.6bvhan6

steps.title: We used Nikon's NIS Elements software (v5
steps.instruction: We used Nikon's NIS Elements software (v5.02.01, Build 1270) to segment pig vagus nerve micrographs stained with Masson's trichrome using the General Analysis RGB tool.

steps.title: We converted the binary segmented image...
steps.instruction: We converted the binary segmented image into “Graticule Masks”, binary images saved as TIFs.

steps.title: We imported the TIFs into Matlab...
steps.instruction: We imported the TIFs into Matlab and generated a data structure of the x and y coordinates of the pixels for each closed boundary of the loaded binary images using the bwboundaries function.

steps.title: We scaled the pixel coordinates to...
steps.instruction: We scaled the pixel coordinates to microns using the segmented scale bar.

steps.title: For each image, we selected preprocessing...
steps.instruction: For each image, we selected preprocessing steps, such as smoothing and sharpening.

steps.title: For each image, we selected ranges...
steps.instruction: For each image, we selected ranges of hues, saturations, and intensities to values that identify the endoneurium (fascicles) and different values to identify the entirety of the nerve.

steps.title: For each image, we selected postprocessing...
steps.instruction: For each image, we selected postprocessing steps, such as setting a minimum size criterion (eliminate small off-target regions), smoothing, cleaning, closing, and filling holes.

steps.title: We made manual adjustments as needed,...
steps.instruction: We made manual adjustments as needed, including manual deletion of off-target regions and filling of target areas that had not been captured.

steps.title: We calculated cross-sectional area of each...
steps.instruction: We calculated cross-sectional area of each fascicle and nerve using Matlab’s polyarea. Effective diameter (for a nerve or fascicle) is the diameter of the circle that has the same cross-sectional area as the raw trace.
